title: Cross-browser UI review: 3-engine matrix with separable baseline approval
problem: The Cloverleaf UI Reviewer (reference-impl/prompts/ui-reviewer.md) runs Playwright exclusively against the chromium engine. This means engine-specific rendering and accessibility-tree differences — present in webkit and firefox, which together represent a material share of production traffic — are never surfaced before a task is merged. Additionally, visual baselines under .cloverleaf/baselines/ are silently overwritten whenever compareVisual encounters a new-baseline or dimension-mismatch result, making baseline changes invisible as a distinct review concern. There is no workflow step that lets a human sign off on baseline drift separately from the underlying code change; both are bundled into the final-gate approval, reducing confidence that visual changes are intentional.
solution: Extend the UI Reviewer to a 3-browser matrix (chromium, webkit, firefox) on all diff-affected routes by: (1) adding a 'browsers' list to reference-impl/config/ui-review.json, defaulting to ["chromium"] for backward compatibility with the full matrix as the recommended value; (2) updating the UI Reviewer prompt to iterate browsers as an outer loop wrapping the existing viewport × route loops, with per-browser escalation when a required browser binary is absent — the escalation message names the missing engine and the install command ('npx playwright install webkit firefox'; on Linux also 'npx playwright install-deps webkit'); (3) adopting per-browser subdirectories for baselines: .cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png — this preserves the existing {slug}-{viewport}.png convention within each subdirectory, keeps git diffs scannable by engine, and eliminates slug/browser token ambiguity; a one-time migration task git-mv's the existing flat chromium baselines into baselines/chromium/; (4) running axe-core on a single configurable engine only, controlled by an 'axe.browser' config key in ui-review.json defaulting to 'chromium' — webkit and firefox contribute to visual-diff coverage only; axe is excluded from non-chromium runs by design because engine-specific DOM, CSSOM, and a11y-tree differences produce false-positive divergence (color-contrast via getComputedStyle, aria-required-children, scrollable-region-focusable) that axe-core does not reliably suppress across Blink, WebKit, and Gecko; (5) introducing a baseline-approval gate implemented as a reference-impl-only sidecar file .cloverleaf/runs/{taskId}/ui-review/state.json containing {'baselines_pending': true} whenever any new-baseline or dimension-mismatch result is produced; the cloverleaf-ui-review skill reads this file after the agent completes and, if baselines_pending is true, leaves the task in ui-review status and surfaces a human-facing message; a new 'approve-baselines' action (human-triggered, reference-impl skill) writes baselines_pending: false and allows the ui-review → qa transition to proceed; no Standard state-machine or schema changes are required; (6) capping total browser combinations at a configurable 'maxCombinations' key in ui-review.json defaulting to 90 (routes × viewports × browsers); when the computed product exceeds the cap the reviewer sorts affected routes by diff size (most-changed first), processes the first floor(maxCombinations / (viewports × browsers)) routes, and emits one 'warning'-severity finding per skipped route with rule 'ui-review-cap'; at the default cap of 90 the worst-case wall clock is approximately 155 s (~2.5 min), within a 5-minute agent-turn budget; (7) updating install.sh and install documentation to run 'npx playwright install webkit firefox' (and 'npx playwright install-deps webkit' on Linux) alongside the existing chromium step — the aggregate additional disk footprint is approximately 300–350 MB (webkit ~180–220 MB + firefox ~90–130 MB) on top of the existing ~300 MB chromium footprint, totalling ~600–650 MB per machine under PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=~/.cache/ms-playwright.
acceptance_criteria: A toy change to a site route causes the UI Reviewer to launch chromium, webkit, and firefox in sequence, producing per-browser baseline PNGs under .cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/ and surfacing any engine-specific visual diffs in the findings.

Axe-core accessibility scanning runs on exactly the configured 'axe.browser' engine (default: chromium) and the axe viewport list, with no duplicate or false-positive findings from multi-engine axe runs; webkit and firefox runs produce no axe output.

Baseline approval can be granted by a human independently of final-gate task approval — a task cannot advance from ui-review to qa while .cloverleaf/runs/{taskId}/ui-review/state.json contains baselines_pending: true; the approve-baselines action clears this flag and unblocks the transition.
Removing one browser from the 'browsers' config list causes that engine's baselines and findings to be skipped without affecting results for remaining browsers.

When a required browser binary is absent, the UI Reviewer returns verdict 'escalate' with a per-browser diagnostic message naming the missing engine and the install command ('npx playwright install webkit firefox'; on Linux additionally 'npx playwright install-deps webkit').

The total number of browser × route × viewport combinations is bounded by the 'maxCombinations' config key (default 90); combinations beyond the cap are skipped and reported as 'warning'-severity findings with rule 'ui-review-cap', with the skipped route name and the current vs cap count in the message.

Existing flat chromium baselines ({slug}-{viewport}.png) are migrated to .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/{slug}-{viewport}.png as an explicit git mv step in the implementation task; no stale flat files remain after migration.
All changes are contained within reference-impl (prompt, config, lib, install.sh); no modifications to the Standard schemas or state-machine JSON are required.
The approved Plan decomposes into discrete implementable tasks targeting reference-impl v0.6.
out_of_scope: CI-level parallelisation of browser runs across workers or containers
Mobile-browser emulation beyond the existing mobile, tablet, and desktop viewport matrix
Live-production testing (review remains preview-server driven, as today)
Modifying the Standard task state-machine to add new states for baseline approval
Cross-browser testing of the Cloverleaf site itself (this RFC targets the UI Reviewer tooling, not a specific site)
Running axe-core on webkit or firefox engines (cross-engine axe runs produce engine-sourced false positives and are excluded by design)
